text.title: aarrr-pirate-metrics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for aarrr-pirate-metrics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Tracking more than one metric per AARRR stage.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multiple metrics per stage create analysis paralysis and diffuse ownership. Each stage needs one accountable number; secondary metrics are context, not targets.

failure-mode.description: Focusing investment on acquisition when activation or retention is broken.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every new user acquired into a broken funnel is waste multiplied. Fix the weakest stage first; the downstream revenue impact is proportional to acquisition volume.

failure-mode.description: Defining activation as "created profile" or another setup action that does not predict retention.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Optimizing for setup completion inflates activation rate while the product's retention remains flat. The only valid activation definition is an action empirically correlated with D30 retention.

failure-mode.description: Not segmenting AARRR metrics by acquisition channel or cohort.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Aggregate metrics mask variation. A strong paid-search cohort can hide poor organic retention; blending them produces a misleading average that leads to wrong investment decisions.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring the referral stage until late in product maturity.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Referral programs take 3-6 months to generate meaningful signal. Starting instrumentation and basic mechanics early means the data is available when it is needed for scaling decisions.
